description: FR13 bundles multiple distinct behaviors into one requirement: (1) hash-based staleness detection for internal docs, (2) time-based staleness detection for edge docs, (3) SUSPECT flagging, (4) one-hop propagation constraint, (5) human/verifier-gated update rule. These are 4-5 separate testable behaviors.
evidence: 'System can detect staleness using two modes: hash-based for internal documents (compare `derives_from` content hash to current upstream hash) and time-based for edge documents (compare research date to domain-specific freshness windows). Stale downstream documents are flagged as SUSPECT, not auto-updated — one-hop propagation only, human/verifier-gated at each level.'
suggestion: Split FR13 into: FR13a (hash-based staleness for internal docs), FR13b (time-based staleness for edge docs), FR13c (SUSPECT flagging on stale documents), FR13d (one-hop propagation, human-gated updates).

location: Functional Requirements > Enforcement & Quality Governance > FR23
description: FR23 bundles model routing configuration, the cognitive hazard rule, and a three-tier model escalation strategy into a single requirement.
evidence: 'Developer can configure model routing defaults per skill and agent via `model:` and `effort:` frontmatter, with a documented default strategy: Sonnet 4.6 at medium effort for general tasks; upgrade to Opus for complex reasoning, orchestration, or outputs without automated validation (cognitive hazard rule: invisible errors from cheaper models cost more than the flagship premium); downgrade to Haiku for well-constrained tasks with downstream validation'
suggestion: Split into FR23a (model/effort frontmatter configuration) and FR23b (documented default model routing strategy with cognitive hazard rule).

location: Functional Requirements > Research & Knowledge Management > FR44
description: FR44 describes multi-model MCP research capability, which is listed as a Growth Feature in the Product Scope section. FR44 is not phase-tagged, creating an ambiguous scope boundary.
evidence: FR44: 'Developer can conduct multi-model research using MCP-integrated LLM providers (Gemini, GPT, and additional providers)'. Growth Features: '**Gemini MCP server** (PT-028) — automated multi-model research via `@rlabs-inc/gemini-mcp`'
suggestion: Tag FR44 as [Growth] or add a phase column to the FR tables.

description: FR29 requires cross-story pattern detection but provides no threshold or trigger condition, making it not testable.
evidence: 'System can detect cross-story patterns in the findings ledger and surface systemic issues'
suggestion: Add threshold: e.g., 'System can detect when the same finding category appears in 3 or more distinct stories and surface it as a systemic pattern.'

location: Functional Requirements > Specification & Development Workflow > FR11
description: FR11 uses 'may improve' which makes it untestable. A requirement describing a probabilistic outcome cannot be verified as pass/fail.
evidence: 'Developer can ask follow-up questions during any workflow step, and the agent treats questions as discovery opportunities that may improve the specification'
suggestion: Rephrase: 'Developer can ask follow-up questions during any workflow step; the agent investigates whether the question reveals a specification ambiguity and, if so, proposes a spec update.'
